checks.finding: Every AC reference in T-001 through T-006 resolves to AC-001 through AC-014 in acceptance-tests.md.
All six tasks declare Blockers as either None or canonical comma-separated T-NNN identifiers; blockers_format_valid is true.
REQ-001 through REQ-012 are each referenced by at least one of T-001 through T-006.
AC-001 through AC-014 are each referenced by at least one task Requirements field.
Every task T-001 through T-006 references at least one valid REQ-NNN identifier.
No task is an exclusively test-file task or has a title containing test, spec, or verify; implementation tasks that include tests explicitly reference applicable AC identifiers.
In attempt 3 round 1, all tasks remain Approval: Draft and Status: Planned; no task is agent-approved or has an invalid status.
T-001 through T-005 pair Risk: high with tdd, and T-006 pairs Risk: medium with acceptance-first; precheck workflow_match_precheck is PASS.
Each AC identifier is assigned to exactly one task, so no same-level primary acceptance target is duplicated.
Every blocker reference resolves to a dependency-graph node among T-001 through T-006.
The graph edges T-003→T-001/T-002, T-004/T-005→T-003, and T-006→T-004/T-005 form an acyclic dependency graph.
Each task has one coherent concern: transition repair, registry/migration records, portable validation, repository/CI integration, quality/review-gate integration, or release metadata.

Each task names inspectable commands, suite outcomes, fixture behavior, verification logs, or implementation reports; T-001 through T-005 include Red-to-Green log paths and T-006 names validation commands and its report path.
traceability.md contains Task Mapping entries for T-001 through T-006, and all mapped requirement identifiers resolve to REQ-001 through REQ-012 in requirements.md.
